_readme: ics_feeds: any public iCal/ICS calendar feed (venue, city, church, library — look for a 'Subscribe/Export .ics' link); events land in live-events.json nightly. category must be one of: music, food, arts, outdoors, sports, family, market, nightlife, festival — but it is only a FALLBACK now: if the feed ships a CATEGORIES line per event (any site running The Events Calendar does), ics_category() in fetch_events.py maps that per-event instead. Best sniff test for a WordPress calendar is <site>/events/?ical=1; if that 404s, try /wp-json/tribe/events/v1/events. Check the response content-type is text/calendar — several DFW sites answer 200 with an HTML page, and some (dallasfarmersmarket.org) serve real ICS but rate-limit hard behind Wordfence, so a feed that works once can 403 for the rest of the day. prekindle_pages: local venues that sell through Prekindle — their public listing page (prekindle.com/events/<slug>) embeds schema.org JSON-LD we parse nightly; to add a venue, find its slug on prekindle.com and add {slug, venue, area}. eventbrite_locations: Eventbrite city discovery pages (eventbrite.com/d/<slug>/all-events/), whose HTML embeds a schema.org ItemList of Events — their public search API was retired, so this is the only key-free route. 20 events per page; "pages" controls how deep to walk each city. Add a city as {slug, pages} using Eventbrite's own slug format (tx--city-name). seated_artists: artists who sell direct through seated.com — its API is keyed by ARTIST, not city, so we walk this watchlist and keep only their DFW-area dates. To add one: open the artist's tour page, view source, copy the UUID from data-artist-id="...", and add {artist, id, category}. Best used for artists who are NOT on Ticketmaster; anything that is on TM will already arrive there and get deduped away. rss_feeds: DFW publication article feeds shown in the site's press wire (headlines, not event rows). CultureMap Dallas and Visit Dallas publish no machine-readable feeds as of 2026-07; their events can arrive via the Google Sheet or any ICS they add later.

do214._note: Do214 (DoStuff Media) DFW calendar, key-free JSON at do214.com/events/<Y>/<M>/<D>.json?page=N. Sorted by popularity, so max_pages is a QUALITY knob as much as a rate limit — page 1 is the best of the night. days is capped by DAYS_AHEAD (30). Requests per run = days x max_pages, throttled by delay_ms; raising either lengthens the nightly job and grows live-events.json, which every page view downloads.
do214._disabled_because: VERIFIED 2026-07-21: the endpoint returns 403 to every non-browser User-Agent (no UA, our UA, Python-urllib, any string with a contact URL) and 200 only to 'Mozilla/5.0'. That is a deliberate bot block, so the ONLY way to run this is to spoof a browser UA — i.e. evade an access control on a company we want a feed/partnership from. Not worth the legal or commercial exposure. Do NOT flip this to true by adding a fake UA. Flip it to true only after Do214/DoStuff Media grants access; the parser below is written and tested against their real payload, so it should work as-is. Contact them about a partner feed first. (robots.txt does NOT disallow /events — but the WAF overrides that as the operative signal.)
